mnemonics.title: Search Algorithm Properties
mnemonics.content:
BFS: Breadth First Search - Complete & Optimal but Memory Heavy
DFS: Depth First Search - Memory Efficient but Not Complete
UCS: Uniform Cost - Optimal but Blind
A*: A Star - Complete, Optimal & Uses Heuristic
GBFS: Greedy Best - Fast but Not Optimal nor Complete

mnemonics.title: A* Conditions
mnemonics.content:
For A* to be optimal:
ADMISSIBLE = Never overestimate
CONSISTENT = Triangle inequality
f(n) = g(n) + h(n) is non-decreasing along any path

mnemonics.title: Minimax Player Types
mnemonics.content:
MAX: Wants to MAXimize score (AI player)
MIN: Wants to MINimize score (Human player)
Alpha: MAX's best option
Beta: MIN's best option
PRUNE when Alpha >= Beta

acronyms.usage: Explores all nodes at current depth before going deeper, guaranteed shortest path in unweighted graphs
Explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking, memory efficient but may not find solution
Informed search using f(n)=g(n)+h(n), optimal if heuristic is admissible
Expands node closest to goal using h(n) only, not guaranteed optimal
Expands lowest cost path, optimal for weighted graphs without heuristic
Combines BFS completeness with DFS memory efficiency
Game theory algorithm for optimal play in two-player zero-sum games

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a tree growing outward from root. BFS = ripples in water expanding uniformly. DFS = drilling a single deep well before trying other wells.
Think of heuristic as a compass pointing toward goal. In 8-puzzle, Manhattan distance is like measuring straight-line distance on a map ignoring obstacles.
Imagine cutting branches off a tree that you know won't produce better fruit than what you already have.
Think of climbing a mountain in fog - you can only see nearby terrain and always move upward toward the peak.
f(n) = g(n) + h(n) is like: distance traveled so far (gas used) + estimated distance remaining (GPS estimate).

analogies.analogy: BFS is like flooding a maze with water from start - water reaches all adjacent cells before going further. DFS is like a single explorer following one path until dead-end then backtracking.
Heuristic is like GPS navigation - it estimates the remaining distance to destination, helping choose which road to take.
Checking only important moves in chess - if you already found a good capture, you can skip analyzing moves that are clearly worse.
Looking for the highest hill in fog by always walking uphill - you might reach a small hilltop and stop, missing the actual highest mountain.
A truth-teller friend who never overestimates - they might underestimate (be pessimistic) but never promise less than reality.
